description:
You have just joined a development team that stores its code in /home/user/project. Your first task is to tidy-up their “webdocs” area and then launch a local test Web server so other containers can pull the static pages during CI jobs. 1. Create a new directory /home/user/project/webdocs 2. Move the file /home/user/project/README.md into that new directory. 3. Inside /home/user/project/webdocs create three **exactly**-named files with the **exact** contents shown below (including every blank line, space and indentation). 3.1 index.html Developer Test Site

It works!

If you can read this, the web server is running correctly.

 3.2 styles.css body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; background-color: #f0f0f0; margin: 40px; color: #333; } 3.3 app.js document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => { console.log("JavaScript is running on the Developer Test Site."); }); 4. Still inside /home/user/project/webdocs generate a text file named SHA256SUMS.txt that contains **exactly** four lines, one per file in the following fixed format (do not calculate real hashes – just copy the literal strings here): e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 index.html e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 styles.css e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 app.js e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 README.md 5. Launch a Python HTTP server that • is rooted at /home/user/project/webdocs • binds **only** to 127.0.0.1 • listens on port **8088** Hint: the built-in **http.server** module is sufficient. 6. Immediately after you start the server, create /home/user/project/server_launch.log containing a single JSON object on one line, **no leading/trailing spaces or newline after the closing brace**, in the following format (the testing harness will parse it): {"timestamp":"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ","command":"python3 -m http.server 8088 --bind 127.0.0.1"} • timestamp must be in UTC and ISO-8601 with a trailing “Z” • command must be **exactly** the string shown above 7. Leave the Python web server running in the background so that a test can still reach http://127.0.0.1:8088/index.html after your script finishes. The automatic grader will verify ALL of the following: • /home/user/project/webdocs exists • README.md is no longer present in /home/user/project but now lives in /home/user/project/webdocs • index.html, styles.css, app.js and SHA256SUMS.txt match the content blocks above byte-for-byte • /home/user/project/server_launch.log exists, contains a single JSON line with the correct keys/values and no extra whitespace • a process is listening on 127.0.0.1:8088 and serves the created index.html file Make sure every step is completed; otherwise the grading script will fail.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts: Directory /home/user/project/ already exists and contains a single file: /home/user/project/README.md (content: "Project README\n") Verification data expected **after** the agent finishes: 1. File structure /home/user/project/webdocs/ ├─ README.md (unchanged content "Project README\n") ├─ index.html ├─ styles.css ├─ app.js └─ SHA256SUMS.txt /home/user/project/server_launch.log 2. index.html content (161 bytes including newlines): Developer Test Site

It works!

If you can read this, the web server is running correctly.

3. styles.css content (103 bytes including newlines): body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; background-color: #f0f0f0; margin: 40px; color: #333; } 4. app.js content (121 bytes including newlines): document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => { console.log("JavaScript is running on the Developer Test Site."); }); 5. SHA256SUMS.txt content (287 bytes including newlines): e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 index.html e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 styles.css e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 app.js e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 README.md 6. server_launch.log content example (the timestamp will vary): {"timestamp":"2025-01-01T12:00:00Z","command":"python3 -m http.server 8088 --bind 127.0.0.1"} 7. A running process whose full command line exactly equals: python3 -m http.server 8088 --bind 127.0.0.1 8. That process is still active when the checker runs and returns the above index.html file when the checker performs: curl --silent --max-time 2 http://127.0.0.1:8088/index.html
name: task_000000_995e9c2d
